verdicts.note: Evidence shows two MCP server repositories: tkhattar14/whatsapp-business-mcp and ampcome-mcps/whatsapp-mcp. Both are community-maintained MCP implementations for WhatsApp Business Cloud API.
The URL https://github.com/tkhattar14/whatsapp-business-mcp is confirmed in the evidence as a valid MCP server implementation with 50+ tools across 8 modules.

Evidence from dmhub.ai and ozonetel.com confirms developers can self-serve via Meta Developer portal + Business Manager to create app, WABA, verify phone number, and generate access tokens without needing a BSP.

Evidence confirms the 2-5 business day Meta Business Verification requirement, free service conversations with paid template messages, and that BSP use is optional (Path A: direct Cloud API vs Path B: BSP platform).
Evidence from dmhub.ai confirms Meta system user/app access tokens via Meta Business Manager and that developers call graph.facebook.com/v{version}/{phone_number_id}/messages endpoints with tokens.
Evidence confirms Cloud API is a REST interface accessed via graph.facebook.com endpoints.
Evidence describes messaging (text, media, interactive, templates), phone number/WABA management, and webhooks - consistent with 'medium' breadth scoped to WhatsApp Business messaging use cases.

Evidence confirms the 2-5 day Meta Business Verification requirement, WABA/phone number setup, webhook verification needs, and template approval workflow - supporting 'buildable-with-caveats' classification.
Evidence explicitly confirms Meta Business Verification takes 2-5 business days and is required before full API access, along with WABA/phone setup and template approval workflow.

Evidence confirms Cloud API is Meta-hosted, self-serve accessible via Meta Developer portal, uses OAuth/token auth via Meta Graph API, supports messaging types mentioned, and requires business verification.
Evidence describes WhatsApp Business API as a customer engagement/messaging channel for business communication, consistent with 'Communications and Messaging' category.
The evidence references Meta's Graph API and WhatsApp Cloud API documentation, consistent with developers.facebook.com/docs/whatsapp being the official developer resource.

Evidence confirms: (1) 2-5 business day verification requirement, (2) multiple community MCP servers exist (tkhattar14 and ampcome-mcps repos) with no Meta-official MCP confirmed, (3) July 2025 pricing overhaul confirms per-template pricing model with free service conversations.
0.75 confidence is reasonable given comprehensive evidence on setup process, pricing, API access, and existing MCP implementations. Minor uncertainty around evolving features and pricing details.

revised.name: WhatsApp Business
revised.one_liner: WhatsApp Business Cloud API lets developers self-serve messaging (text, media, templates, interactive messages) via Meta's Graph API using OAuth/token-based access after business verification.

auth_methods.notes: Meta system user / app access tokens generated via Meta Business Manager / Developer app; standard Meta Graph API OAuth token flow.
Long-lived access tokens/system user tokens used directly against graph.facebook.com/v{version}/{phone_number_id}/messages endpoints.

revised.self_serve_notes: Developers can self-serve via Meta Developer portal + Business Manager: create app, WABA, verify phone number, and generate access tokens without needing a BSP. No monthly platform fee for Cloud API itself; billing is per delivered template message (marketing/utility/auth) with 1,000 free service conversations/month. Requires Meta Business Verification (2-5 business days) which adds friction but is still self-serve, not gated behind sales/partnership approval. Alternative is using a BSP for added convenience/markup.

api_surface.notes: Meta Graph API (Cloud API) exposes messaging (text, media, interactive, templates), phone number/WABA management, webhooks for inbound messages/status. Scoped to WhatsApp Business messaging use cases, not a broad general-purpose API.

revised.main_blocker: Requires Meta Business Verification (2-5 day approval process) and WABA/phone number setup before API access is usable; developer must handle webhook verification, rate limits, and template approval workflow themselves for direct Cloud API access.

evidence.supports: Describes self-serve setup via Meta Business Manager, WABA creation, phone verification, and direct Cloud API access with own credentials, plus verification requirement.
Confirms Cloud API is Meta-hosted, developer-ready REST interface with pricing model (free service conversations, paid template messages) and two deployment paths (direct or via BSP).
Existing open-source MCP server implementation for WhatsApp Business Cloud API.
Additional existing MCP server exposing WhatsApp Cloud API messaging functionality as tools.

flags: Requires Meta Business Verification (2-5 business days) before full API access, which may block instant self-serve for agent onboarding
Multiple community MCP servers exist but no official Meta-maintained MCP server confirmed
Pricing is usage-based (per template message) rather than flat, adding complexity for cost estimation
